sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 19.61 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.19)

statement.latex: Let$=\{ _r\ |\ r \}$be an elementary carpet of type$$over a commutative ring$K$(see 7.28), and let$( )= x_r( _r)\ |\ r$be its carpet subgroup. Define the closure of the carpet~$$to be the set of additive subgroups${ }=\{ { }_r\ |\ r \},$where${ }_r=\{t K\ |\ x_r(t) ( )\}.$Is the closure${ }$of a carpet$$always a carpet? An affirmative answer is known if$=A_l, D_l, E_l$.

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
